message.content:
Update the file /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/CLAUDE.md to reflect 4 spec file renames. The files have already been git mv'd.

Changes needed:
1. `RBSA-SpecTop.adoc` → `RBS0-SpecTop.adoc` and the mapping key `RBSA` → `RBS0`
2. `JJSA-GallopsData.adoc` → `JJS0-GallopsData.adoc` and the mapping key `JJSA` → `JJS0`  
3. `BUSA-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc` → `BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc` and the mapping key `BUSA` → `BUS0`
4. `VOS-VoxObscuraSpec.adoc` → `VOS0-VoxObscuraSpec.adoc` — VOS doesn't have a separate mapping key in the acronym table (it's listed as `VOS`), so update it to `VOS0`

Also search for and update any OTHER inline references to these old filenames anywhere in CLAUDE.md (there may be references in managed sections, JJK configuration, etc.).

IMPORTANT: Only update filename references (like `RBSA-SpecTop.adoc` or `JJSA-GallopsData.adoc`). Do NOT change conceptual uses like "RBSA mapping section" in prose — those refer to the section inside the document, not the filename. Similarly "VOS" as a system name stays "VOS". Also do NOT change `RBSAA` or similar — those are different files.

Read the file first, then make precise edits.

content.text: Let me read the file first to understand all the references that need updating.

   126→- **SRFC**    → `srf-SRFC-StudyRaftConcepts.adoc`
   127→- **ABG**     → `wrs-ABG-AccordBuilderGuide.md`
   128→- **ALTL**    → `wrs-ALTL-AccordLogicalTaskLens.claudex`
   129→- **PMTL**    → `wrs-PMTL-ProtocolMachineryTaskLens.claudex`
   130→- **SDTL**    → `wrs-SDTL-ShapeDesignTaskLens.claudex`
   131→- **TITL**    → `wrs-TITL-TestInfrastructureTaskLens.claudex`
   132→- **TLG**     → `wrs-TLG-TaskLensGuide.md`
   133→- **WRC**     → `wrs-WRC-WardRealmConcepts.adoc`
   134→- **WCC**     → `WCC-WebClaudetoClaudeCode.md`
   135→
   136→## Working Preferences
   137→- When user mentions an acronym, immediately navigate to the corresponding file
   138→- Assume full edit permissions for all files in the three main directories
   139→- For bash scripts, prefer functional programming style with clear error handling
   140→- For .adoc files, maintain consistent AsciiDoc formatting
   141→- For .claudex files, preserve the specific format requirements
   142→
   143→### Heredoc Delimiter Selection
   144→
   145→When generating heredocs for stdin content, the delimiter must not appear alone on any line within the content.
   146→
   147→- **Check content first**: If content includes `EOF` (e.g., code examples showing heredoc patterns), use a different delimiter
   148→- **Safe alternatives**: `SPEC`, `CONTENT`, `DOC`, `PACESPEC`, `SLASHCMD`
   149→- **Pattern**: `cat <<'DELIM' | command` (quoted delimiter prevents variable expansion)
   150→
   151→### AsciiDoc Linked Terms
   152→When working with .adoc files using MCM patterns:
   153→- **Linked Term**: Concept with three parts:
   154→  - Attribute reference: `:prefix_snake_case:` (mapping section)
   155→  - Replacement text: `<<anchor,Display Text>>` (what readers see)
   156→  - Definition: `[[anchor]] {attribute}:: Definition text` (meaning)
   157→- Definitions may be grouped in lists or dispersed through document
   158→- Maintain consistent prefix categories (e.g., `mcm_`, `rbw_`, `gad_`)
   159→- Use snake_case for anchors, match attribute to anchor
   160→
   161→### Forbidden Shell Operations
   162→
   163→**Never use `cd` in Bash commands — NO exceptions.**
   164→
   165→The working directory persists between Bash tool calls. A single `cd` corrupts ALL subsequent commands that use relative paths, including every `./tt/` tabtarget.
   166→
   167→- Use absolute paths instead of cd'ing
   168→- Use `--manifest-path` or equivalent flags
   169→- This applies to ALL work — not just Rust builds
   170→
   171→**There is no safe cd.** Do not reason that "I'll cd back" — the next tool call may be yours or another officium's, and it will break.
   172→
   173→### Rust Build Discipline
   174→
   175→Tabtargets for Rust operations (run from project root):
   176→- `tt/vow-b.Build.sh` → `cargo build --manifest-path Tools/vok/Cargo.toml`
   177→- `tt/vvw-r.RunVVX.sh <cmd>` → runs vvx binary with arguments
   178→- `tt/vow-t.Test.sh` → `cargo test --manifest-path Tools/vok/Cargo.toml`
   179→
   180→See **Forbidden Shell Operations** above — never `cd`, use `--manifest-path` instead.
   181→When running cargo directly, use `--manifest-path` to stay at project root.
   182→
   183→## Prefix Naming Discipline ("mint")
   184→
   185→When asked to "mint" names, apply these rules. Full study: `Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`
   186→
   187→### Two Universes
   188→
   189→**Primary Universe** — ANY persistent identifier, regardless of where it lives. This includes the obvious (code, docs, functions, variables, directories) AND the easy-to-miss: git refs, slash commands, environment variables, paths in target repos, configuration keys. **If it's a name that persists, it's in scope.** Prefixes must be globally unique and respect terminal exclusivity.
   190→
   191→**Tabtarget Universe** — launchers in `tt/`. These are *colophons* referencing the primary universe. `rbw-` points to the `rbw` workbench; it doesn't consume new prefix space.
   192→
   193→### Core Rules
   194→
   195→**Rule 1 - Project Prefix**: Names start with 2-4 char project ID:
   196→`rb` (Recipe Bottle), `gad` (GAD), `bu` (BUK), `jj` (Job Jockey), `pb` (Paneboard), `mcm`/`axl` (CMK), `crg`, `wrs`, `ccc` (CCCK), `hm` (HMK), `lmci`, `vsl` (VSLK)
   197→
   198→**Rule 2 - Terminal Exclusivity**: A prefix either IS a name or HAS children, never both.
   199→- `rbg` has children (`rbga`, `rbgb`...) → `rbg` cannot name a thing
   200→- `rbi` names Image module → `rbia`, `rbib` forbidden
   201→
   202→### Primary Universe Patterns
   203→
   204→| Domain | Pattern | Example |
   205→|--------|---------|---------|
   206→| Code files | `prefix_Word.ext` | `rbga_ArtifactRegistry.sh` |
   207→| Doc files | `ACRONYM-Words.ext` | `RBSA-SpecTop.adoc` |
   208→| Functions (public) | `prefix_name()` | `buc_log_args()` |
   209→| Functions (private) | `zprefix_name()` | `zbuc_color()` |
   210→| Variables | `PREFIX_NAME` | `BURC_PROJECT_ROOT` |
   211→| AsciiDoc attributes | `:prefix_term:` | `:rbw_depot:` |
   212→| AsciiDoc anchors | `[[prefix_term]]` | `[[rbw_depot]]` |
   213→| Directories | `prefix/` | `Tools/buk/` |
   214→
   215→### Tabtarget Universe Pattern
   216→
   217→Tabtargets follow: `{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
   218→
   219→Colophons must reference valid Primary Universe prefixes. See **BUK Concepts** below for terminology (colophon, frontispiece, imprint, workbench).
   220→
   221→### Extended Namespace Checklist
   222→
   223→When minting, enumerate ALL namespaces the system touches:
   224→
   225→| Namespace | Pattern | Example |
   226→|-----------|---------|---------|
   227→| Git refs | `refs/{prefix}/...` | `refs/vvg/locks/*` |
   228→| Slash commands | `/{prefix}-{noun}` | `/vvc-commit` |
   229→| Command files | `.claude/commands/{cmd}.md` | `vvc-commit.md` |
   230→| Environment vars | `{PREFIX}_NAME` | `VVG_SIZE_LIMIT` |
   231→| Target repo paths | `Tools/{kit}/...` | `Tools/vvk/bin/vvx` |
   232→
   233→This is not exhaustive. The principle: **any persistent name anywhere is in the mint universe.**
   234→
   235→### Kit Infrastructure Suffixes
   236→
   237→**Scoped to kit development** (VOK, VVK, JJK, CGK, etc.) — not universal:
   238→
   239→| Suffix | Type | Suffix | Type |
   240→|--------|------|--------|------|
   241→| `*a_` | Arcanum | `*k` | Kit directory |
   242→| `*b_` | suBagent | `*l_` | Ledger |
   243→| `*c-` | slash Command | `*r` | Rust binary |
   244→| `*g_` | Git utilities | `*t_` | Testbench |
   245→| `*h_` | Hook | `*w_` | Workbench |
   246→
   247→Within kit prefixes, these constrain the tree. If `*c_` means Command, don't use `vvc_` for "Commit".
   248→
   249→**Other domains have their own conventions:**
   250→- AsciiDoc concept attributes (`:prefix_term:`) follow MCM semantic categories
   251→- Domain-specific suffixes may evolve per project
   252→
   253→### Minting Workflow
   254→
   255→Before minting new prefixes:
   256→1. **Enumerate namespaces** — list every place this name will appear (code, refs, commands, env vars, target paths...)
   257→2. **Check reserved suffixes** — ensure the suffix matches intended type
   258→3. **Verify terminal exclusivity** — search existing trees, check the memo
   259→4. **Document the allocation** — add to prefix map in relevant heat/spec
   260→
   261→### Project Prefix Registry
   262→
   263→| Prefix | Project |
   264→|--------|---------|
   265→| `rb` | Recipe Bottle |
   266→| `gad` | GAD (Google AsciiDoc Differ) |
   267→| `bu` | BUK (Bash Utilities Kit) |
   268→| `jj` | Job Jockey |
   269→| `pb` | Paneboard |
   270→| `mcm`, `axl` | CMK (Concept Model Kit) |
   271→| `crg` | Config Regime |
   272→| `wrs` | Ward Realm Substrate |
   273→| `ccc` | CCCK (Claude Code Container Kit) |
   274→| `hm` | HMK (Hard-state Machine Kit) |
   275→| `lmci` | LMCI (Language Model Console Integration) |
   276→| `vsl` | VSLK (Visual SlickEdit Local Kit) |
   277→
   278→For expanded prefix trees within each project, see **File Acronym Mappings** above.
   279→
   280→## Common Workflows
   281→1. **Bash Development**: Start with relevant utility (BUC/BUD/BUT/BUV/BUW), check dependencies
   282→2. **Requirements Writing**: Open spec file, review related documents in same directory
   283→
   284→## Bash Utility Kit (BUK) Concepts
   285→
   286→BUK provides tabtarget/launcher infrastructure. Key vocabulary:
   287→
   288→| Term | Definition |
   289→|------|------------|
   290→| **Tabtarget** | Launcher script in `tt/` that delegates to a workbench |
   291→| **Colophon** | Routing identifier (includes hyphen): `rbw-B`, `buw-tt-ll` |
   292→| **Frontispiece** | Human-readable description (PascalCase): `ConnectBottle` |
   293→| **Imprint** | Optional target parameter: `nsproto`, `srjcl` |
   294→| **Zipper** | BCG-compliant module kindling colophon→module→command array constants |
   295→| **Workbench** | Routes commands: `{prefix}w_workbench.sh` |
   296→| **Testbench** | Routes tests: `{prefix}t_testbench.sh` |
   297→
   298→**Tabtarget pattern**: `{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
   299→
   300→- The `.` is the delimiter between parts
   301→- The hyphen is part of the colophon (not a separator)
   302→- Colophon naming follows **Prefix Naming Discipline** above
   303→
   304→Full spec: `Tools/buk/README.md`
   305→
   306→<!-- MANAGED:CMK:BEGIN -->
   307→## Concept Model Kit Configuration
   308→
   309→Concept Model Kit (CMK) is installed for managing concept model documents.
   310→
   311→**Configuration:**
   312→- Lenses directory: `lenses`
   313→- Kit path: `Tools/cmk/README.md`
   314→- Upstream remote: `OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM`
   315→
   316→**Concept Model Patterns:**
   317→- **Linked Terms**: `{category_term}` - references defined vocabulary
   318→- **Attribute References**: `:category_term: <<anchor,Display Text>>` - in mapping section
   319→- **Anchors**: `[[anchor_name]]` - definition targets
   320→- **Annotations**: `// ⟦content⟧` - Strachey brackets for type categorization
   321→
   322→**Available commands:**
   323→- `/cma-normalize` - Apply full MCM normalization (haiku)
   324→- `/cma-render` - Transform to ClaudeMark (sonnet)
   325→- `/cma-validate` - Check links and annotations
   326→- `/cma-prep-pr` - Prepare upstream contribution
   327→- `/cma-doctor` - Validate installation
   328→
   329→**Subagents:**
   330→- `cmsa-normalizer` - Haiku-enforced MCM normalization (text, mapping, validation)
   331→
   332→For full MCM specification, see `Tools/cmk/MCM-MetaConceptModel.adoc`.
   333→
   334→**Important**: Restart Claude Code session after installation for new commands and subagents to become available.
   335→
   336→<!-- MANAGED:CMK:END -->
   337→
   338→## Current Context
   339→- Primary focus: Recipe Bottle infrastructure and tooling
   340→- Architecture: Bash-based CLI tools with Google Cloud integration
   341→- Documentation format: AsciiDoc (.adoc) for specs, Markdown (.md) for guides
   342→- Public project page: https://scaleinv.github.io/recipebottle
   343→
   344→<!-- When editing MANAGED:JJK content, also update: Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/vocjjmc_core.md -->
   345→<!-- MANAGED:JJK:BEGIN -->
   346→## Job Jockey Configuration
   347→
   348→Job Jockey (JJ) is installed for managing project initiatives.
   349→
   350→**Concepts:**
   351→- **Heat**: Bounded initiative with coherent goals that are clear and present (3-50 officia). Status: `racing` (active execution) or `stabled` (paused for planning). Location: `current/` or `retired/` (done).
   352→- **Pace**: Discrete action within a heat; can be bridled for autonomous execution via `/jjc-pace-bridle`
   353→- **Itch**: Future work (any detail level), lives in jji_itch.md
   354→- **Scar**: Closed work with lessons learned, lives in jjs_scar.md
   355→- **Spook**: Team infrastructure stumble — any workflow failure improvable with deft attention. Capture as a pace when encountered, don't lose the current thread.
   356→
   357→**Identities vs Display Names:**
   358→- **Firemark**: Heat identity (`₣AA` or `AA`). Used in CLI args and JSON keys.
   359→- **Coronet**: Pace identity (`₢AAAAk` or `AAAAk`). Used in CLI args and JSON keys.
   360→- **Silks**: kebab-case display name. Human-readable only — NOT usable for lookups.
   361→
   362→When a command takes `<firemark>` or `<coronet>`, provide the identity, not the silks.
   363→
   364→- Target repo dir: `.`
   365→- JJ Kit path: `Tools/jjk/README.md`
   366→
   367→**JJ Slash Command Reference:**
   368→
   369→ALWAYS read the corresponding slash command before attempting JJ operations.
   370→
   371→**CRITICAL**: JJK CLI syntax is non-standard. Do NOT guess based on common CLI conventions.
   372→- Dockets go via stdin, not `--docket`
   373→- Positioning uses `--move X --first`, not `--position first`
   374→- **Verb names are NOT jjx_ subcommands**: there is no `jjx_slate`, `jjx_mount`, `jjx_bridle`, `jjx_notch`, `jjx_groom`, etc. Slash commands wrap differently-named jjx_ commands. Read the slash command to find the actual CLI invocation.
   375→- Read the slash command to see the exact `./tt/vvw-r.RunVVX.sh jjx_*` invocation pattern.
   376→
   377→**Heredoc for stdin**: Use `cat <<'DELIM' | jjx_*` pattern with quoted delimiter. The delimiter must not appear alone on any line in the content — if content shows heredoc examples with `EOF`, use a different delimiter like `DOCKET` or `PACESPEC`.
   378→
   379→| When you need to... | Read first |
   380→|---------------------|------------|
   381→| Add a new pace | /jjc-pace-slate |
   382→| Refine pace docket | /jjc-pace-reslate |
   383→| Bridle for autonomous execution | /jjc-pace-bridle |
   384→| Mark pace complete | `jjx_close` |
   385→| Commit with JJ context | /jjc-pace-notch |
   386→| Execute next pace | /jjc-heat-mount |
   387→| Review heat plan | /jjc-heat-groom |
   388→| Evaluate bridleable paces | /jjc-heat-quarter |
   389→| Reorder paces | /jjc-heat-rail |
   390→| Add steeplechase marker | /jjc-heat-chalk |
   391→| Pause or resume heat | /jjc-heat-furlough |
   392→| Draft paces between heats | /jjc-heat-restring |
   393→| Retire completed heat | /jjc-heat-retire |
   394→| View heat or pace | `jjx_show` |
   395→
   396→**Quick Verbs** — When user says just the verb, invoke the corresponding command:
   397→
   398→| Verb | Noun | Command |
   399→|------|------|---------|
   400→| muster | heats | `jjx_list` |
   401→| parade | heat/pace | `jjx_show` |
   402→| scout | heats | `jjx_search` |
   403→| braid | heats | /jjc-heat-braid |
   404→| nominate | heat | `jjx_create` |
   405→| mount | heat/pace | /jjc-heat-mount |
   406→| groom | heat | /jjc-heat-groom |
   407→| quarter | heat | /jjc-heat-quarter |
   408→| rail | heat | /jjc-heat-rail |
   409→| furlough | heat | /jjc-heat-furlough |
   410→| retire | heat | /jjc-heat-retire-dryrun |
   411→| garland | heat | /jjc-heat-garland |
   412→| restring | heat | /jjc-heat-restring |
   413→| slate | pace | /jjc-pace-slate |
   414→| reslate | pace | /jjc-pace-reslate |
   415→| bridle | pace | /jjc-pace-bridle |
   416→| notch | pace | /jjc-pace-notch |
   417→| wrap | pace | `jjx_close` |
   418→
   419→**CLI Command Reference:**
   420→
   421→```
   422→jjx_show [TARGET] [--detail] [--remaining]
   423→jjx_list [--status racing|stabled|retired]
   424→jjx_create --silks SILKS
   425→jjx_enroll FIREMARK --silks SILKS [--first|--before C|--after C] <stdin
   426→jjx_reorder FIREMARK [--move C --first|--last|--before C|--after C | CORONET...]
   427→jjx_alter FIREMARK [--racing|--stabled] [--silks SILKS]
   428→jjx_record CORONET FILE [FILE...] [--intent "msg"]
   429→jjx_close CORONET
   430→jjx_log FIREMARK [--limit N]
   431→jjx_search PATTERN [--actionable]
   432→jjx_archive FIREMARK [--execute]
   433→jjx_transfer FIREMARK --to FIREMARK <stdin
   434→jjx_continue FIREMARK
   435→jjx_mark CORONET --marker M --description "text"
   436→jjx_paddock FIREMARK [<stdin for set]
   437→jjx_relocate CORONET --to FIREMARK
   438→jjx_orient [FIREMARK]
   439→jjx_revise_docket CORONET <stdin
   440→jjx_arm CORONET <stdin
   441→jjx_relabel CORONET --silks "name"
   442→jjx_drop CORONET
   443→jjx_get_brief CORONET
   444→jjx_get_coronets FIREMARK [--rough]
   445→jjx_landing CORONET AGENT <stdin
   446→jjx_validate [--file PATH]
   447→```
   448→
   449→**Composition Recipes:**
   450→
   451→Common command combinations using `&&`:
   452→
   453→```bash
   454→# Revise docket and rename:
   455→jjx_revise_docket C <docket_stdin && jjx_relabel C --silks "name"
   456→
   457→# Bridle with revised docket:
   458→jjx_revise_docket C <docket_stdin && jjx_arm C <warrant_stdin
   459→```
   460→
   461→**Key points:**
   462→- `jjx_show` takes firemark OR coronet directly as sole argument — NO flags for target selection
   463→  - Heat overview: `jjx_show AF`
   464→  - Single pace: `jjx_show AFAAb`
   465→  - Valid flags: `--detail`, `--remaining` only
   466→- NEVER invent CLI flags — run `--help` first or read the slash command
   467→- `jjx_orient` output includes `pace_coronet` (next actionable pace) — no separate show call needed to find what's next
   468→
   469→**Commit Discipline:**
   470→When working on a heat, ALWAYS use `/jjc-pace-notch` for commits. NEVER use `jjx_record` directly — the slash command handles context detection. Never bypass heat/pace affiliation and steeplechase tracking.
   471→
   472→Notch handles two cases:
   473→- **Pace-affiliated**: Active pace provides context (coronet in commit)
   474→- **Heat-affiliated**: No active pace, but commit is part of heat work (firemark only)
   475→
   476→When user says "notch", invoke `/jjc-pace-notch` — don't second-guess based on pace state.
   477→
   478→**Multi-Officium Discipline:**
   479→Multiple Claude officia (concurrent git-activity streams, not sessions — see VOS `vost_officium`) may work concurrently in the same repo. The explicit file list in `/jjc-pace-notch` enables orthogonal commits.
   480→
   481→- Claude is **additive only** — make commits, never discard changes
   482→- "Unexpected" uncommitted changes are likely another officium's work
   483→- If something looks wrong, ASK — do not "fix" by discarding
   484→- Commit only YOUR files; ignore everything else
   485→
   486→**Forbidden Git Commands — NO exceptions, NO "safe" variants:**
   487→- `git reset` — ALL forms: `--hard`, `--soft`, `--mixed`, with paths, without paths. Even `git reset HEAD <file>` (unstaging) is forbidden — it's too close to destructive variants and Claude will reason its way into worse forms.
   488→- `git restore` — ALL forms: working tree, staged, with `--source`, without
   489→- `git checkout <file>` — when used to discard changes (navigating branches is fine)
   490→- `git clean` — ALL forms
   491→- `git stash` — ALL forms
   492→
   493→**What to do instead:**
   494→- Staging wrong? Just run `/jjc-pace-notch` with the correct file list — it handles staging
   495→- Made a mistake? Make a new commit that fixes it — additive, not destructive
   496→- Confused by repo state? ASK the user — another officium may be mid-work
   497→- Need to undo something? Explain the situation to the user and let them decide
   498→
   499→**Build & Run Discipline:**
   500→Always run these after Rust code changes:
   501→- `tt/vow-b.Build.sh` — Build
   502→- `tt/vvw-r.RunVVX.sh` — Run VVX
   503→
   504→**JJX Commands Are Self-Committing:**
   505→`jjx_enroll`, `jjx_close`, `jjx_record`, and other state-mutating jjx commands commit gallops changes internally. Do NOT follow them with a separate `vvx_commit` — the tree will already be clean. If a commit command says "Nothing to commit", check `git status --short` and accept the result.
   506→
   507→**Diagnose Before Escalating:**
   508→When a command fails, check the simplest explanation first. "Nothing to commit" means the tree is clean — verify with `git status`, don't try creative workarounds. "Unexpected argument" means wrong CLI syntax — read `--help`, don't guess. One diagnostic command beats three speculative retries.
   509→
   510→**Important**: New commands are not available in this installation session. You must restart Claude Code before the new commands become available.
   511→
   512→### Bridleability Assessment
   513→
   514→When evaluating whether a pace is ready for autonomous execution (bridled state), apply these criteria:
   515→
   516→**Bridleable** (can bridle for autonomous execution) — ALL must be true:
   517→- **Mechanical**: Clear transformation, not design work
   518→- **Pattern exists**: Following established pattern, not creating new one
   519→- **No forks**: Single obvious approach, not "we could do X or Y"
   520→- **Bounded**: Touches known files, not "find where this should go"
   521→
   522→**NOT bridleable** (needs human judgment):
   523→- Language like "define", "design", "architect", "decide"
   524→- Establishing new patterns others will follow
   525→- Multiple valid approaches requiring human choice
   526→- Scope unclear or requires judgment calls
   527→
   528→**Examples:**
   529→- ✓ Bridleable: "Rename function `getCwd` to `getCurrentWorkingDirectory` across codebase"
   530→- ✓ Bridleable: "Add error handling to `fetchUser` following pattern in `fetchOrder`"
   531→- ✗ Not bridleable: "Define KitAsset struct and registry pattern" (design decisions)
   532→- ✗ Not bridleable: "Improve performance of dashboard" (unclear scope, many approaches)
   533→
   534→### Wrap Discipline
   535→
   536→**NEVER auto-wrap a pace.** Always ask the user explicitly: "Ready to wrap ₢XXXXX?" and wait for confirmation before running `jjx_close`. The user decides when work is complete, not the agent.
   537→
   538→This applies to:
   539→- Direct work on rough paces
   540→- Spawned agents executing bridled paces
   541→- Any situation where work appears "done"
   542→
   543→When work is complete, report outcomes and ask. Do not wrap.
   544→
   545→When wrapping (after user confirms), always pipe a one-line summary of the work via stdin:
   546→`echo "Added bitmap displays to orient output" | jjx_close CORONET`
   547→The agent always has context about what was accomplished — include it.
   548→
   549→<!-- MANAGED:JJK:END -->
   550→
   551→<!-- MANAGED:BUK:BEGIN -->
   552→## Bash Utility Kit (BUK)
   553→
   554→BUK provides tabtarget/launcher infrastructure for bash-based tooling.
   555→
   556→**Key files:**
   557→- `Tools/buk/buc_command.sh` — command utilities
   558→- `Tools/buk/bud_dispatch.sh` — dispatch utilities
   559→- `Tools/buk/buw_workbench.sh` — workbench
   560→
   561→**Tabtarget pattern:** `{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
   562→
   563→For full documentation, see `Tools/buk/README.md`.
   564→
   565→<!-- MANAGED:BUK:END -->
   566→
   567→<!-- MANAGED:VVK:BEGIN -->
   568→## Voce Viva Kit (VVK)
   569→
   570→VVK provides core infrastructure for Claude Code kits.
   571→
   572→**Key commands:**
   573→- `/vvc-commit` — Guarded git commit with size validation
   574→
   575→**Key files:**
   576→- `Tools/vvk/bin/vvx` — Core binary
   577→- `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` — Installation brand file
   578→
   579→For installation/uninstallation, use `vvi_install.sh` and `vvu_uninstall.sh`.
   580→
   581→<!-- MANAGED:VVK:END -->
   582→
